title: Zero authentication enforcement anywhere in the request path
section: Protected routes / middleware; Whether Supabase Auth is actually used
dependencies: None - never implemented, comment says TEMP
root_cause: Active middleware explicitly disables all protection ('TEMP: allow everything while stabilizing app')
business_impact: Any unauthenticated visitor can load every CRM page (leads, proposals, revenue, settings, admin)
technical_impact: No defense-in-depth at the framework level; every page relies on nothing
risk_of_fix: Medium - must not break legitimate logged-in flows or public pages (chat widget, proposal share links, webhook)
regression_risk: High - touches every page in the app
testing_required: Full manual click-through of every route logged-in and logged-out; automated route-guard test once tests exist

title: 26 of 31 API routes perform no session/auth check at all
affected_files: src/app/api/**/route.ts (26 of 31 files - see routes.csv auth_check=NO)
dependencies: ISS-001 (shared auth helper should be built once)
root_cause: No auth check was ever added to these routes; only 4 routes were individually hardened
business_impact: Any client can read/write leads, proposals, payments, campaigns, analytics without logging in
technical_impact: API is the actual data boundary since RLS is bypassed by service-role client (ISS-011) - this is the only real access control layer, and it is absent
risk_of_fix: Medium-High - must distinguish intentionally-public routes (chat, webhook, share, health, track-view) from ones that need auth
testing_required: Per-route: authenticated request succeeds, unauthenticated request rejected (401); regression test on the intentionally-public allowlist
rollback_required: YES - per-route revert, ship behind allowlist config

title: Admin role check compares the wrong field - likely always denies or never denies
dependencies: ISS-016 (user_profiles table must exist and be queryable first)
root_cause: getCurrentUser() returns the raw Supabase Auth User object; its .role is the GoTrue role, never joined against user_profiles.role
business_impact: Admin user-management panel is either completely inaccessible to real admins or (worse) fails open depending on GoTrue's default role value
technical_impact: Broken authorization logic on the one route that has a role check at all
risk_of_fix: Low - isolated to one file, well-understood fix (join user_profiles before comparing)
testing_required: Manual: log in as seeded admin and non-admin, verify 200/403 respectively; automated test once framework exists

title: WhatsApp webhook POST handler has no signature/HMAC verification
root_cause: X-Hub-Signature-256 verification was never implemented
business_impact: Anyone who discovers the webhook URL can forge inbound WhatsApp messages, trigger auto-replies, create fake leads/conversations
technical_impact: Webhook trusts all input; feeds directly into leads/conversations tables
risk_of_fix: Medium - must not break the real Meta webhook handshake or message delivery while adding verification
regression_risk: Medium - webhook is external-facing, hard to fully test without Meta sandbox
testing_required: Manual test with valid and forged signatures against a Meta test app; regression test on legitimate message flow
rollback_required: YES - can ship signature check behind a flag that logs-but-doesn't-reject first

title: No input validation (zod) on any of the 31 API routes
root_cause: zod was added as a dependency but never wired in; only ad hoc manual checks exist on some routes
business_impact: Malformed/malicious payloads can reach Supabase writes and the Auth admin-create-user call unvalidated
technical_impact: No schema boundary between untrusted input and business logic/DB writes
risk_of_fix: Medium - must define correct schemas without breaking existing valid payloads from the frontend
testing_required: Per-route: valid payload still works, invalid payload now returns 400 with a clear message
rollback_required: YES - schema validation can be added route-by-route, each independently revertible

title: 28 of 31 API routes use the service-role Supabase client, bypassing RLS entirely
section: Service-role vs session-scoped vs anon client, by route; Row Level Security
dependencies: ISS-002 (route-level auth must exist before RLS can be meaningfully relied upon as defense-in-depth)
root_cause: Architectural choice, likely made to avoid dealing with per-user RLS policies during rapid feature development
business_impact: RLS policies defined in the database are almost entirely decorative in practice - the API route code is the only real access boundary, and most of it has none (ISS-002)
technical_impact: Single-tier trust model; any route bug is a full-database bug
risk_of_fix: High - re-architecting data access is invasive and touches every route
testing_required: Full regression pass on every route after any client-switch; cannot be done piecemeal safely
rollback_required: YES - must be planned as a dedicated phase with rollback per route

title: notification_settings table has a policy but Row Level Security was never enabled on it
root_cause: ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY statement for this table was never written in any of the 9 migrations
business_impact: VIP threshold, daily-summary phone number, and other operational config are fully open to anyone with the anon key
technical_impact: Policy is a documented no-op; table has effectively no access control
risk_of_fix: Low - single ALTER TABLE statement, additive, low blast radius
testing_required: Anon-key read/write attempt before and after; confirm service-role access unaffected
rollback_required: YES - trivial to disable RLS again if something breaks

title: conversations table has an anonymous UPDATE policy with no row scoping (USING TRUE)
root_cause: Policy was written permissively, likely to let the anonymous chat widget update its own conversation, but with no session/ownership scoping
business_impact: Any anonymous client can overwrite any customer's conversation record, including extracted PII fields and escalation state
technical_impact: Data integrity and confidentiality gap on a customer-data table
risk_of_fix: Medium - must identify the legitimate use case (chat widget updating its own session) and scope the policy without breaking it
testing_required: Chat widget end-to-end test (create + update own conversation should still work); attempt to update a different session_id's conversation (should now fail)
rollback_required: YES - policy change is a single reversible migration

title: Five tables/views used by live API routes do not exist in any migration file
section: Tables/views referenced in application code but ABSENT from every migration
root_cause: Either undocumented manual schema changes in the live Supabase project (drift from this repo), or these features have never worked
business_impact: Admin user management, proposal invoicing, payment recording, receipts, and lead import may all be silently broken in production right now
technical_impact: Repo cannot be used to stand up a working database from scratch; 'install and start using it' is not currently possible
risk_of_fix: Low to write the missing migration IF the live schema is known; Medium if schema must be reverse-engineered from the live DB first
testing_required: Query live Supabase project's information_schema to capture actual column definitions; test each affected route end-to-end after migration is written
phase: Phase 3 (live DB inspection is a prerequisite)

title: leads.lead_stage column is used extensively in code but never created by any migration
affected_files: src/app/api/dashboard/stats/route.ts; src/app/api/leads/hot/route.ts; src/app/api/proposals/intelligence/route.ts; src/modules/leads/lead-stage-manager.ts; src/modules/leads/types.ts; src/modules/automation/escalation-engine.ts; supabase/migrations/010_phase5_proposal_intelligence.sql (view reference only, line 200)
section: Tables/views referenced in application code but ABSENT from every migration
root_cause: Same as ISS-009 - either schema drift or a genuinely broken feature
business_impact: Dashboard stats, hot-leads view, and proposal intelligence all read this column; if truly absent, these features return null/incorrect data silently
technical_impact: Core lead-pipeline feature (stage tracking) may not function at all
risk_of_fix: Low once the correct column definition is confirmed against the live DB
testing_required: Confirm column exists in live DB via information_schema; if missing, write migration and verify dashboard/stats and leads/hot render real stage data afterward
rollback_required: YES - additive column, safe to add
phase: Phase 3 (live DB inspection is a prerequisite)

title: Migration 009 is missing from the sequence (gap between 008 and 010)
root_cause: Unknown - either a migration was never committed, was deleted, or the number was intentionally skipped
business_impact: Confusing for any new developer reconstructing schema history; may indicate lost work
technical_impact: Migration history is not self-describing/complete
risk_of_fix: Low - investigation only, no code risk
estimated_effort: Small (investigation) + unknown if a real migration must be reconstructed
testing_required: Check git log/history for any reference to a 009 migration; check live DB migration tracking table if Supabase migration history is used

title: leads_needing_followup view is defined three separate times across migration history
root_cause: Iterative patching without removing earlier definitions from migration history
business_impact: None currently (006 version wins, is correct) - purely a maintainability/clarity issue for future migration authors
technical_impact: Migration history is misleading about the 'true' current definition without reading all three
risk_of_fix: Low
testing_required: Confirm live view matches the 006 definition before/after any cleanup

title: Duplicate 007_missing_tables.sql exists outside supabase/migrations/
root_cause: Leftover from past troubleshooting
business_impact: None functionally (migration tooling only reads supabase/migrations/)
technical_impact: Repo clutter, risk of someone editing the wrong copy
risk_of_fix: Low
testing_required: Confirm the two files are still identical, then remove the stray copy; confirm build/migrations still work

title: Hardcoded personal/business phone number baked directly into migration seed data
root_cause: Convenience during initial setup, never moved to a config UI or env var
business_impact: Changing the daily-summary recipient number requires a new migration or manual DB edit instead of a settings-page update
technical_impact: Config value embedded in schema history instead of runtime-configurable data
risk_of_fix: Low

title: Entire secondary WhatsApp subsystem (6 files, ~1020 LOC) has zero importers - fully dead code
affected_files: src/lib/whatsapp/auto-responder.ts; src/lib/whatsapp/conversation-manager.ts; src/lib/whatsapp/detect-source.ts; src/lib/whatsapp/lead-resolver.ts; src/lib/whatsapp/send-message.ts; src/services/whatsapp/process-inbound.ts
root_cause: A more sophisticated conversation state-machine was built to replace the simple keyword-matching auto-reply, but was never wired into the live webhook route, and targets tables (whatsapp_messages, whatsapp_conversations) that were never migrated
business_impact: None currently (dead code doesn't run) - but represents significant unrealized engineering investment, and is a trap for future maintainers who might import it assuming it works
technical_impact: Confusing architecture; two competing WhatsApp implementations; whichever is kept must absorb the other's logic
risk_of_fix: Decision-dependent: Low if deleted, Medium-Large if finished and wired in (needs new migration for its target tables)

title: src/lib/ai-summary.ts is fully implemented but unreachable - its API route is a stub
root_cause: Route was scaffolded but never finished/wired to the library
business_impact: Daily AI summary, WhatsApp delivery of it, and automatic VIP-lead flagging never run - a documented feature simply does not happen
technical_impact: Dead code sitting one function call away from working
risk_of_fix: Low - the hard part (the logic) is already written and typed

title: src/modules/automation/escalation-engine.ts has zero importers anywhere in the codebase
root_cause: Written but never wired into any route or scheduled job
business_impact: Automated escalation of stalled/high-value leads to human staff does not happen despite the logic existing
technical_impact: Dead business-critical automation
risk_of_fix: Medium - need to decide where it should be invoked from (a route, a cron job, or inline in lead-update paths)

title: src/modules/followups/followup-engine.ts and followup-rules.ts have zero importers anywhere
root_cause: Cadence rules and message-building logic written but never invoked; the live /api/followups and /api/cron/followups routes do not import them (per routes.csv, cron/followups touches follow_ups/leads tables directly without these modules)
business_impact: Follow-up cadence (how often and what to say per lead temperature) is not actually driving the automated follow-up system as designed
technical_impact: Dead business-critical automation, parallel/competing logic may already exist inline in the cron route

testing_required: Compare current cron behavior vs. this module's intended behavior before switching; regression test on follow-up scheduling after the switch

title: src/modules/leads/lead-stage-manager.ts is reachable only through a dead, misnamed route file
root_cause: File should be named route.ts but isn't - simple oversight
business_impact: Lead pipeline stage transitions (the state machine that should prevent invalid stage jumps) have no live HTTP entry point; whatever UI drives stage changes today is either using a different unvalidated path or stage changes aren't persisted with validation at all
technical_impact: Core CRM pipeline-integrity logic is completely bypassed
risk_of_fix: Low - rename the file, confirm route registers, confirm it's actually called from the frontend where stage changes happen
testing_required: PATCH the route with a valid transition (expect 200 + DB update) and an invalid transition per VALID_TRANSITIONS (expect 400)

title: Two API route files are misnamed and therefore never registered as Next.js routes

business_impact: lead-stage-route.ts: see ISS-019. proposal share route: currently harmless because the live share page bypasses it and queries Supabase directly client-side with the anon key - but that means the share flow has zero server-side gating
technical_impact: Dead code / inconsistent architecture (one feature server-gated, the other purely RLS-gated client-side)
risk_of_fix: Low for the rename itself; decision needed on whether to keep the proposal-share route or formalize the direct-client-query approach
testing_required: Confirm each route responds correctly once renamed; confirm no naming collision in the same folder

title: Three empty literal brace-glob directories plus one empty stray directory clutter src/app/api
root_cause: A past script ran mkdir with an unexpanded shell brace-glob, creating literal directories instead of the intended set of separate folders (evidenced by all_routes.txt at repo root)
business_impact: None functionally (Next.js ignores non-matching directories)
technical_impact: Filesystem clutter, confusing to new developers browsing the API tree

title: Two conflicting middleware.ts files exist (root and src/)
dependencies: ISS-001 (fix the active one first, then remove the dead duplicate)

business_impact: None directly (Next.js ignores the root one) but actively misleading to any developer who edits the wrong file expecting it to take effect; root file also logs every API request via a stray console.log if ever mistakenly activated
technical_impact: Confusing dual source of truth for a security-critical file
risk_of_fix: Trivial once ISS-001 is fixed

title: Two independent OAuth/magic-link callback implementations diverge in behavior
dependencies: ISS-008 (one of the two failure paths is already broken)
root_cause: Two callback routes were built at different times/for different link types and never consolidated; they use different redirect-param names (redirect vs next) and different failure targets
business_impact: Confusing for any client generating auth links - which URL should be used depends on undocumented knowledge of which implementation is 'correct'
technical_impact: Duplicate cookie-handling logic (one reuses supabase-server.ts, the other reimplements inline) - a fix to session handling in one might not propagate to the other
risk_of_fix: Medium - need to determine which callers use which route before consolidating
testing_required: Test both magic-link and OAuth flows against whichever route(s) are kept; confirm no caller breaks

title: OAuth failure redirect target does not exist (404)
root_cause: Page was presumably planned but never built
business_impact: A user whose magic-link/OAuth exchange fails sees a raw 404 instead of a helpful error page
technical_impact: Missing error-state UX
risk_of_fix: Low - either build the page or redirect to /auth/login?error=... consistent with the other callback implementation
testing_required: Trigger a failed code exchange (expired/invalid code) and confirm a sensible page renders

title: ADMIN_EMAIL / ADMIN_PASSWORD are declared but completely unused
root_cause: Real auth (Supabase Auth) superseded whatever these were originally meant for, and the old declarations were never removed
business_impact: A new developer setting up the project will fill these in believing they're load-bearing, when they do nothing
technical_impact: Misleading setup documentation
risk_of_fix: Trivial
testing_required: Confirm zero usage one more time immediately before removal

business_impact: None functionally today

title: Two Tailwind config files exist with divergent font-family definitions

business_impact: Whichever file Next.js actually resolves determines the live typography - the other is silently dead, and it's not verifiable from static inspection which one wins

risk_of_fix: Low - determine the active one via a visible test change, then delete the other
testing_required: Change a font in one file, rebuild, visually confirm which file's change took effect, then remove the loser

title: Server Actions CSRF origin check is disabled for all origins
root_cause: Comment states 'Vercel handles security' - an incorrect assumption; this is an application-level relaxation, not a platform guarantee
business_impact: Any origin can invoke Server Actions on behalf of a logged-in user's browser session (CSRF exposure), compounding ISS-001/002
technical_impact: Removes a built-in Next.js protection for no documented reason
risk_of_fix: Low - restrict to actual production/preview origins
regression_risk: Low-Medium (must enumerate every legitimate origin including Vercel preview URLs)
testing_required: Confirm Server Actions still work from the real app domain and Vercel preview deployments after restricting origins

title: No security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.) configured anywhere
root_cause: Never added
business_impact: Increased exposure to clickjacking, XSS impact severity, and other browser-side attacks that security headers mitigate
technical_impact: Missing standard production hardening
risk_of_fix: Low-Medium - CSP in particular requires careful tuning to avoid breaking inline scripts/styles/third-party embeds (fonts, chat widget)
regression_risk: Medium (CSP is easy to get wrong and silently break functionality)
testing_required: Full manual click-through of every page after headers are added, checking browser console for CSP violations

title: 77 explicit escapes from TypeScript's strict mode across the codebase
affected_files: 53x `: any` + 22x `as any` + 2x `<any>`, spread across src/ (see CODE_METRICS.md for full grep); spot-verified: src/lib/sheets.ts:360, src/app/api/proposals/email/route.ts:19
root_cause: Convenience during rapid development; tsconfig strict:true was set but not enforced via lint rule (no @typescript-eslint no-explicit-any)
business_impact: None directly, but each occurrence is a place where TypeScript cannot catch a type error at compile time
technical_impact: Erodes the safety net that strict:true is supposed to provide
risk_of_fix: Low per-occurrence, but 77 occurrences is a meaningful body of work to type correctly
regression_risk: Low per-fix, cumulative regression risk if rushed
testing_required: Type-check (tsc --noEmit) after each batch of fixes; no runtime behavior should change if done correctly

title: No centralized environment variable configuration or startup validation
affected_files: 18 distinct env vars read directly via process.env.* across 21 files (only SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is centralized, via src/lib/supabase.ts)

business_impact: App fails at first request to whichever route needs a missing var, rather than failing fast at startup with a clear message - directly blocks the 'install and immediately start using it' goal from the original project brief
technical_impact: No single source of truth for what configuration the app requires
risk_of_fix: Medium - building a central config module and migrating 21 files to use it is real work, but mechanical and low-risk if done file-by-file
testing_required: Confirm app still boots and each feature still works after each file is migrated to the central config

title: Environment variable names in .env.example do not match what the code actually reads

title: Proposal 'email' feature does not send email - it returns a mailto: link
root_cause: No SMTP/transactional email provider was ever integrated; a mailto: fallback was built instead and the route's own response says so explicitly
business_impact: Any workflow assuming proposals are emailed to clients automatically is not actually happening server-side - it silently depends on a human opening the returned mailto_url in their own email client
technical_impact: Feature gap relative to what the CRM name/scope implies (project brief explicitly lists 'Email communication' as a required capability)

regression_risk: Low (new code path, doesn't touch existing behavior if additive)

title: Unawaited fire-and-forget database write with a swallowed error
root_cause: Convenience - the response doesn't wait on a non-critical status update
business_impact: If the update silently fails, the proposal's email_sent_at/status fields go out of sync with reality, with zero visibility
technical_impact: Silent data inconsistency, hard to debug after the fact
risk_of_fix: Low - await it and log (don't necessarily need to fail the request) on error
testing_required: Force the update to fail (e.g. bad proposal_id) and confirm the error is now at least logged

title: Two independent, non-shared implementations of the same Meta Graph API call exist

business_impact: None currently (one path is dead)

title: Business contact phone numbers and pricing hardcoded across 6+ library files instead of centralized
affected_files: src/lib/ai.ts (lines 32,37,48,50,249); src/lib/campaigns.ts (lines 35,47,49,50,140,160); src/lib/documents.ts (lines 82,96,104,123,150); src/lib/proposal-intelligence.ts (lines 113,121); src/lib/proposal-pdf.ts (line 549); src/lib/templates.ts (lines 121,163,177,212,218,253)

title: proposal-pdf.ts fetches Google Fonts live over the network at PDF-render time
root_cause: Convenience - simplest way to get custom fonts into generated HTML/PDF
business_impact: PDF generation latency depends on an external network call every time; if Google Fonts is unreachable or slow, proposal PDF generation degrades or fails for customers
technical_impact: External runtime dependency for a core deliverable (the proposal PDF customers receive)
risk_of_fix: Low - self-host the font files or inline them at build time
testing_required: Generate a proposal PDF with network access to fonts.googleapis.com blocked, confirm current behavior, then confirm fix resolves it

title: CookieItem interface is defined independently in two separate files
root_cause: supabase-server.ts predates supabase-types.ts and was never migrated to import from it
business_impact: None functionally today (both definitions are structurally identical)
technical_impact: Two sources of truth for the same shape; a future edit to one won't propagate to the other
risk_of_fix: Trivial
testing_required: Type-check after consolidating to a single shared import

title: Auto-reply copy contains hardcoded generic hotel pricing that does not match the real seeded banquet packages
affected_files: src/app/api/whatsapp/webhook/route.ts (buildAutoReply function, lines 194-207 per prior recon); supabase/migrations/007_missing_tables.sql (packages table, real prices at lines 170-190)
root_cause: Auto-reply copy appears to be leftover boilerplate from an earlier generic-hotel product direction, never updated after the packages table/pricing was finalized
business_impact: Customers asking the WhatsApp bot about pricing receive incorrect information (wrong venue names, wrong prices) instead of the real Silver/Gold/Platinum packages
technical_impact: Direct customer-facing misinformation from an automated system
risk_of_fix: Low-Medium - rewrite buildAutoReply to query the packages table instead of using hardcoded strings
testing_required: Send a WhatsApp message containing 'price', confirm the auto-reply now quotes real seeded package prices

title: Unverified whether any in-scope CRM page renders hardcoded/mock data instead of live data
affected_files: All 12 pages in src/app/(crm), src/app/admin, src/app/analytics
root_cause: Only a keyword scan for explicit placeholder markers was performed in the audit; full data-fetching logic was not traced per page
business_impact: Unknown until verified - if any page shows mock data, users may be making business decisions off fake numbers
technical_impact: Unknown - flagged for investigation, not confirmed as a real defect
risk_of_fix: N/A until investigated
testing_required: Manually cross-check each page's displayed numbers against direct Supabase queries for the same data

title: Whether the 5 missing tables/1 missing column represent live schema drift or a genuinely broken feature is unresolved
section: Tables/views referenced in application code but ABSENT from every migration
dependencies: None - this IS the prerequisite investigation for ISS-009 and ISS-010
root_cause: Read-only file-system audit cannot connect to the live database
business_impact: Blocks any safe fix to ISS-009/ISS-010 until resolved - fixing blind risks either creating duplicate/conflicting schema (if it already exists) or missing the fix entirely (if it doesn't)
technical_impact: Root-cause ambiguity for two Critical-severity findings
risk_of_fix: N/A - this is an investigation task, not a code change
testing_required: Connect to the live Supabase project (with credentials) and query information_schema.tables and information_schema.columns for the 5 table/view names and the lead_stage column
phase: Phase 0 (must happen before Phase 3 database work)

title: No one-click install / setup wizard / startup env validation exists
affected_files: Entire project - absence confirmed across package.json scripts, DEPLOYMENT_AUDIT.md
dependencies: ISS-036, ISS-037 (env centralization/doc fixes are prerequisites)
root_cause: Never built - project brief's goal of 'install and immediately start using it' was not implemented
business_impact: New deployments require manual trial-and-error to discover required configuration (compounded by ISS-037's doc mismatches)
technical_impact: No fail-fast startup check; errors surface late, per-feature, at request time
risk_of_fix: Medium - build a startup validation script/health check gate plus a setup guide
testing_required: Fresh-clone install attempt following only the setup documentation, timed and logged for gaps

title: Blanket no-store cache header applied to all API routes with no differentiated caching strategy
root_cause: Simplest safe default when the app was young; never revisited for genuinely cacheable reads (e.g. packages, festival_calendar)
business_impact: No functional bug - just leaves performance on the table for low-change, high-read data
technical_impact: Missed opportunity for edge/CDN caching on read-heavy, rarely-changing endpoints
risk_of_fix: Low - opt specific routes into caching, don't change the default
testing_required: Load-test a newly-cached route before/after to confirm expected latency improvement and correct cache invalidation on writes

title: Two page.tsx files exist outside the audited (crm)/admin/analytics scope and were not evaluated
affected_files: src/app/page.tsx (351 lines); src/app/auth/login/page.tsx (151 lines, covered separately in AUTH_AUDIT.md)
section: Pages found outside the requested scope
root_cause: Out of the original audit's literal Part 8 scope
business_impact: Unknown - landing page quality/correctness was never assessed
technical_impact: Coverage gap in the audit itself, not a confirmed application defect
risk_of_fix: N/A until reviewed
testing_required: Manual review of the landing page for correctness/quality akin to the other 12 pages
